Gwynesse Harlaw
Gwynesse by Drazenka Kimpel © Fantasy Flight Games
Allegiance House Harlaw
Culture ironborn
Father Lord Harlaw
Spouse Deceased husband[1]
Books
  • A Feast for Crows (mentioned)
  • A Dance with Dragons (mentioned)

Gwynesse Harlaw is a noblewoman of House Harlaw. She is a sister of Lord Rodrik Harlaw.

Contents

  • 1 Character
  • 2 History
  • 3 Recent Events
    • 3.1 A Clash of Kings
    • 3.2 A Feast for Crows
    • 3.3 A Dance with Dragons
  • 4 Quotes by Gwynesse
  • 5 Quotes about Gwynesse
  • 6 Family
  • 7 References

Character[ ]

Gwynesse is seven years older than her brother Rodrik.[1] She still grieves the loss of her husband in Greyjoy's Rebellion, and has become forgetful.[1]

History[ ]

Gwynesse's husband died in the sea battle off Fair Isle during Greyjoy's Rebellion in 289 AC.[1] Following his death, Gwynesse returned to Ten Towers, where she has remained since. One of the towers of the castle was renamed the Widow's Tower in her honor. She believes that the castle should be hers since she is older than Lord Rodrik Harlaw, and constantly reminds her brother of this.[1]

Recent Events[ ]

A Clash of Kings[ ]

Alannys Harlaw, wife of Lord Balon Greyjoy, has moved to Ten Towers to live with her sister Gwynesse, as Harlaw is less raw than Pyke.[2]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

Lord Rodrik Harlaw tells his niece, Asha Greyjoy, that his sister Gwynesse continues to mourn her husband but has become forgetful.[1]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

After fleeing the kingsmoot on Old Wyk, Asha briefly visits Gwynesse and her mother, Alannys Harlaw, at Ten Towers before sailing for Deepwood Motte. Gwynesse maintains that Ten Towers should belong to her.[3]

Quotes by Gwynesse[ ]

I will only stay until my grief has passed, though by rights Ten Towers should be mine, for I am seven years your elder.[1]

—Gwynesse to Rodrik Harlaw

I am seven years his elder. Ten Towers should be mine.[3]

—Gwynesse to Asha Greyjoy

Quotes about Gwynesse[ ]

Asha: And how fares my aunt?
Rodrik: Still seven years my elder, and convinced Ten Towers should be hers. Gwynesse grows forgetful, but that she does not forget. She mourns for her dead husband as deeply as she did the day he died, though she cannot always recall his name.[1]

—Asha Greyjoy and Rodrik Harlaw
